entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 4 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-04 07:12AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The Wrap: ASX lifts on energy, real estate stocks ahead of RBA meeting
  • NSW floods LIVE updates: Parts of Sydney told to evacuate as floodwaters rise across the city; BOM expects rain to ease later today
  • Baby humpback whale spotted with mum off the Gold Coast
  • Sydney’s census lesson: Bold action needed to tackle housing affordability
  • Aboriginal flag to permanently fly on Melbourne's West Gate Freeway
  • The key factor putting a floor under property prices
  • Unlicensed Melbourne driver who broke police officer's leg avoids jail
  • Freddy explains Wighton omission
  • Beale targets Wallabies return
  • Train dispute risks $246m cost blowout for Sydney Metro
  • Longmuir bristles at focus on Fyfe’s forward shift
  • Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy
  • Cargo ship in trouble off NSW coast in wild weather
  • ‘Surely I was worth a conversation’: The fallout from email break-ups
  • Billy lifts lid on Storm's 'extreme' preparation
  • ‘Change creates opportunity’: Should the AFL look at a fixture overhaul?
  • Ship stranded off Sydney coast
  • Palaszczuk government bans three ‘dual hat’ Labor lobbyists
  • Andrews government predicted to 'easily' win Victorian election in November
  • Anthony Albanese imposes new sanctions on Russia
  • Rates to lift for third month in a row as jobs market tightens further
  • Best of cartoons, July 4, 2022
  • The road to Kyiv: Albanese, Zelensky and the staff with no embassy
  • Australia is doing more to help Ukraine than some NATO members
  • Making businesses stamp out sexual harassment ‘a no-brainer’ for gender equality
  • Europe looks to Australia for green hydrogen amid energy crisis
  • Pace of change emerges as a key challenge for Albanese
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • Best of cartoons, July 3, 2022
  • Chris Dawson had three motives to murder wife Lynette, prosecutor says
  • Body modifier jailed over woman’s death after infection from silicone snowflake implant
  • Government launches legal action to stop rail disruptions
  • Sydney's third flood within months
  • ‘Everybody is confused’: Humanities students hit with surprise fee rises
  • ‘The system is creaking’: Sydney’s housing stress hotspots revealed
  • NSW floods as it happened: Residents told to evacuate as floods hit Sydney
  • NSW reports second diphtheria case in unvaccinated child
  • Roe v Wade isn’t just about the US. Here are the effects around the world
  • ‘New evidence’ to show Trump wanted to join Capitol riot
  • Mountain glacier collapses in the Italian alps
  • Several people killed in Danish mall shooting
  • Prince Charles’ multimillion-dollar property deals under scrutiny
  • Deadly shooting at Copenhagen shopping mall
  • ‘It’s pure terror’: Several dead in Copenhagen shopping centre shooting
  • Mass shooting in Copenhagen shopping centre
  • Glacier collapse in Italy’s Dolomites kills several hikers
  • Russia seizes Lysychansk, pivotal eastern Ukraine city
  • Albanese promises Zelensky new $100m aid package during Kyiv meeting
  • Victim recounts Maxwell’s role in Epstein’s ‘paedophile island’ abuse
  • No fuel, no cash: Sri Lanka keeps its schools closed amid economic meltdown
  • Albanese visits war-ravaged towns in Ukraine
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • United States airshow stung ends in tragedy
  • Judge urged 10-year-old rape victim to stay pregnant
  • Evacuation orders issued in Nepean and Hawkesbury
  • Australia news LIVE: Albanese meets with Zelensky in Ukraine; Thousands of Sydney homes at risk of flooding as heavy rain continues in NSW
  • New footage captures Queensland military disaster
  • Flight disruptions, airport chaos ‘here for the long haul’ as COVID cuts linger
  • Venom from Queensland spiders could be used to treat heart attacks
  • Court’s CCTV footage in spotlight as Jarryd Hayne supporter sues Seven
  • Temperatures drop to 10 degrees below average in Queensland
  • Quirky Melbourne busker is drawing crowds
  • WorkSafe charges St Basil’s over COVID-19 outbreak that killed dozens
  • ‘End of a 13-year boom’: Australia’s largest super fund posts fall for first time since GFC
  • ‘Done deal’: Australians spend their way to another rate rise
  • Suncorp shores up reinsurance as it prepares for NSW floods claims
  • Risky strategy for picking winners and losers in the green economy
  • ‘Baseless’: Australian biotech Cochlear steps up fight against US university over hearing device
  • Bauxite miners want to clear seven Rottnest Islands’ worth of WA forest
  • Seven could save $135m in Big Bash stoush with CA
  • Australia’s resources exports break records amid global energy crisis
  • Halfway point: What people are watching on TV in 2022
  • Borrowers set for another big rate rise as markets sweat economic data
  • #Gentleminions: Why are cinemas full of loud, Minions-loving teens in suits?
  • Teens wear suits for screenings of Minions: The Rise of Gru
  • The world gets worse, but public libraries are forever
  • Adam Goodes shortlisted in new children’s literature award for First Nations stories
  • The top 10 movies you need to watch in July
  • Cargo ship in danger in wild weather off Sydney’s south
  • Cargo ship loses power off coast south of Sydney
  • Nearly 70 flood evacuations in place for Sydney as weather emergency continues
  • Flooding in Lansvale
  • 70 evacuation orders across NSW with more heavy rain to come
  • National weather forecast for Monday July 4
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Latest Western arms will help Ukraine recapture Lysychansk, Zelensky says
  • Ukrainian forces withdraw from Lysychansk; Kyiv says Donbas battle not over
  • At least 3 dead, several injured after shooting at Copenhagen mall
  • Judge asks U.S. if Saudi crown prince should be immune from suit
  • Russia claims key city in punishing conquest of eastern Ukraine
  • How green became the color of abortion rights
  • Wary of China threat, Taiwanese join Ukraine’s fight against Russia
  • Barriers to abortion in Canada make it an unlikely haven for Americans
  • 3 feet of rain sets up 4th round of flood misery for Sydney
  • 4th crew member rescued after ship sinks south of Hong Kong
  • Bus falls into deep gorge in northern India, killing 16
  • US Navy offers cash for tips to seize Mideast drugs, weapons
  • Bonnie grows into a hurricane in Pacific off southern Mexico
  • 3 dead, 3 critically wounded in shooting at Denmark mall
  • Ricky Martin denies restraining order allegations
  • Latest migrant caravan breaks up in Mexico after 2 days
  • Alpine glacier chunk detaches, killing at least 6 hikers
  • Russia claims control of pivotal eastern Ukrainian province
  • Akron police release video of officers shooting Black man dozens of times
  • These high school seniors raced away from graduation to fight a fire
  • The Rainbow Family comes to Colorado, bringing peace, love and anxiety
  • Multiple criminal referrals of Trump possible, Cheney says
  • Inflation is making homelessness worse
  • Bill to grant crypto firms access to Federal Reserve alarms experts
  • Texts, web searches about abortion have been used to prosecute women
  • Why Is It So Hard to Get Wimbledon Tickets?
  • How to Reverse the West’s Creativity Crisis
  • Warren Buffett Has Advice for the Barbarians at Japan’s Gate
  • Your Old Fridge Is Vladimir Putin’s Friend. Dump It!
  • Why Japan Will Struggle to Do Without Russian Energy
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • Nearly every American has a foreboding the country they love is losing its way
  • Don’t venerate or vilify the Founders. Vindicate their radical vision.
  • The court’s EPA ruling was about something much bigger than one agency
  • Why Glenn Youngkin — or someone like him — must run in 2024
  • This July Fourth, we’re torn over what we love about our country
  • This 1972 speech is more relevant than ever this Fourth of July
  • Democracy must not die in darkness
  • Distinguished person of the week: She showed her party what patriotism is
  • July Fourth is a time to sing our complicated country’s praises
  • Lithuania is enforcing E.U. sanctions against Russia
  • An advance for non-heterosexual medical students and health care
  • We can’t lose sight of the climate crisis
  • The mirage of equivalence
  • Solving the gun issue is harder than it seems
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Lovebug outbreak hits northwestern Seoul
  • Yoon appoints JCS chief, Education Minister without hearing
  • Yoon shrugs off latest polls, but should he?
  • Kim Jin-pyo elected National Assembly speaker
  • N. Korea tightens border controls, vigilance on ‘alien things’ near inter-Korean border

  • [Hwang’s China and the World] The end of appeasing North Korea, time to deal with its fundamental interest

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 4 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-04 07:03AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Man, 28, remanded in custody for alleged murder of yoga instructor at 5-star hotel in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong man, 66, arrested for alleged cruelty to 6 rabbits
  • Fourth crewman rescued after ship wrecked off Hong Kong in Typhoon Chaba, but 26 still missing
  • New tech tools are available thanks to the pandemic – now educators are figuring out how to use them together with more traditional activities to build skills relevant to future workplaces
  • How rail giant MTR Corp is moving Hong Kong closer to mainland China
  • ‘Hong Kong, add oil’ should not be taboo, says Canto-pop legend Jacky Cheung after mainland backlash over anniversary greeting
  • Alex Law, famed Hong Kong director and screenwriter behind Echoes of the Rainbow and An Autumn’s Tale, dies at age 69
  • ‘Epoch-making’ author Ni Kuang, who put Asian stamp on science fiction, dies in Hong Kong at age 87
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong lawmaker tests positive 2 days after posing for photo with Xi Jinping during 25th anniversary of handover celebrations
  • Plastic shopping bag charge should be increased to at least HK$2, says Hong Kong environmental group ahead of Legco meeting on levy
  • Chances of survival for 27 missing crew members of typhoon-hit vessel ‘very slim’, Hong Kong rescue authorities admit on day 2 of search
  • Hong Kong police lay holding charge of murder on man suspected of killing 23-year-old yoga instructor at 5-star hotel
  • The Taiwanese musician mapping mainland China’s military sites – one airfield at a time
  • China still weighing up risks of engagement with Afghanistan’s Taliban government, experts say
  • Both G7 and BRICS lack the dominance to resurrect grand rivalries of Cold War power politics
  • Foreign Minister Wang Yi becomes most senior Chinese official to visit Myanmar since coup
  • Former Russian foreign minister warns ‘self-centred’ Nato risks deeper crisis over Ukraine
  • Chinese police officer’s false evidence claims raise fresh concerns about investigation into attack on female diners
  • Former Japanese prime minister urges Tokyo to talk more to China and stop trying to ‘encircle’ it alongside the US
  • Chinese food firm Maiquer under investigation over milk additive
  • After brutal attack, Chinese women are turning to self-defence classes
  • Climate change: melting Tibetan glaciers could unleash potentially dangerous bacteria, scientists say
  • Japan protests as first Chinese navy ship in 4 years spotted near Diaoyu Islands
  • Tourists are returning to Japan’s Kyoto, but not everyone’s happy to see them
  • Why is Malaysia seeing a rise in data leaks, and what is the government doing to stop it?
  • Indonesian historian Onghokham’s ‘fearless’ life as gay Chinese man, Java expert celebrated in biography
  • More Americans are becoming anti-vaxxers as conspiracy theories about Covid-19 vaccines spread
  • UK PM under fire amid claims he knew about ‘sex pest’ lawmaker’s behaviour
  • Hundreds protest in US after release of video showing police killing black man
  • Thousands wounded and five dead in rare Uzbekistan protests: reports
  • Nigeria police rescue dozens, including children, held in church waiting for Christ’s second coming
  • 31 bodies, some decomposing, found at Indiana funeral home
  • Cologne’s Pride parade draws more than 1 million people in Germany
  • Shark kills two tourists from Austria and Romania in Egypt’s Red Sea
  • 3 dead and 4 in critical condition after shooting at Copenhagen shopping centre
  • Six hikers killed, 8 injured after Alpine glacier chunk breaks loose on Italian mountain
  • Greatest-ever number of Russian diplomats to leave Bulgaria, in mass expulsion
  • Man driving jet truck at more than 483kph dies in US air show crash
  • Russia claims control of last major Ukrainian city in eastern Luhansk
  • Legendary British theatre director Peter Brook dies aged 97
  • Swedish company develops plant-based ‘human meat burger’
  • Hong Kong’s New World Group introduces four and a half day work week for better work-life balance
  • China NFTs: Tencent and Ant Group join industry pledge to ban cryptocurrency and fight speculation
  • Climate Change: China Power International Development acquires US$1.12 billion in clean-energy assets, eyeing transition goals
  • ETF Connect to strengthen Hong Kong’s super-connector role and solidify city’s position as Asia’s top ETF trading hub
  • HKEX, Sands China lead losses in Hong Kong as Covid-19 flare-up dents recovery bets while Ganfeng slides on insider trading probe
  • Why stock markets must stop acting like casinos, especially in Asia
  • New World scion’s family office plans equity hedge fund to capture improving market sentiment
  • Result-oriented John Lee must beware of procrastination traps
  • Hong Kong investors gain access to US$89.6 billion in China-based ETFs as expansion of Stock Connect scheme kicks in
  • Ganfeng, China’s largest lithium salts producer, is under probe for suspected insider trading
  • Typhoon dampens property investor spirits as few flats in new Hong Kong housing project find buyers
  • Emerging Asia at risk from EU’s protectionist carbon border tax
  • Empowering teachers to build the classroom of the future
  • What travel in Ukraine is like amid the war – my unusual trip to Odesa
  • The Man Who Sold His Skin movie review: Tunisia’s Oscar nominee is a refugee drama doubling as a contemporary art satire
  • Forget being ‘Fujian people’ or Cantonese – we in Southeast Asian diaspora should see ourselves as citizens who happen to be of Chinese ancestry
  • Are common vitamins and supplements worth the money? They don’t do as much as we think, and some could be damaging, major study suggests
  • Chinese art museum’s European offshoot in Berlin closes, a victim of social and political turbulence and funding issues, its artistic director says
  • Blind Chinese make-up artist teaches thousands of visually impaired women to apply make-up using sense of touch
  • A Star Ferry sunset tour of Hong Kong’s Victoria Harbour aboard converted vessel the World Star – what’s it like?
  • Gender-fluid Miss Universe contestant on having a ‘gay aura’ and why Bhutan should be seen as an LGBTQ-friendly country
  • The next HoYeon Jung? Meet Jang Yoon-ju of Money Heist Korea: the model-turned-actress has starred in Aespa and Lee Hyori’s music videos and is friends with Red Velvet’s Joy and Irene Kim
  • STYLE Edit: Luxury French perfumer Henry Jacques opens its first stand-alone duplex boutique in the heart of Paris, an expansive 4,300 sq ft on the iconic Avenue Montaigne dedicated to haute perfume
  • The warning behind Loewe’s eco-conceptual Paris Fashion Week show: Jonathan Anderson’s spring/summer menswear collection paired TV screen plates with clumps of grass for a dystopic vision
  • Meet the WWE’s new girl boss CEO, Stephanie McMahon: the wife of wrestler Triple H, she was put in charge following disgraced dad Vince McMahon’s misconduct allegations
  • 14 celebrities who became first-time dads after 50, from Elton John and George Clooney, to Simon Cowell, Andy Cohen, David Letterman and Star Wars voice talent James Earl Jones
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘I smoke weed and I go to strip clubs. But I care about people!’ Killer Mike on rapping, politics and race
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: leaders gather to work out ‘Marshall plan’ to rebuild Ukraine; Zelenskiy vows to retake Lysychansk
  • Russia-Ukraine war: what we know on day 131 of the invasion
  • This Fourth of July, it’s worth pondering the true meaning of patriotism | Robert Reich
  • A local’s guide to Lausanne, Switzerland: cafes and beaches on Lake Geneva
  • Pressure points: threat of violence builds as Kenya’s elections approach
  • Amazon Fire 7 2022 review: budget tablet gets design and speed upgrade
  • A new start after 60: ‘I lost my ability to speak – and became a film-maker’
  • Original Sins by Matt Rowland Hill review – electric account of heroin addiction
  • Italian alps glacier collapse: search resumes for missing hikers after six killed
  • ‘Cancer care can’t stop’: flood-hit Assam hospital uses boats to reach patients
  • Fuel from cargo ship stranded off Sydney coast could cause environmental disaster, experts say
  • Europe at risk of recession amid concerns Russia could cut gas supplies
  • It doesn’t matter if Boris Johnson is a dead man walking as PM. The damage is done | Nesrine Malik
  • ‘It felt as though an orgy could erupt at any moment’ – the Pride I’ll never forget
  • ‘Eventually it will just be a barcode, won’t it?’ Why Britain’s new stamps are causing outrage and upset
  • ‘Gods that walk among us’: the photographer bringing glamour to portraits of community leaders
  • Bill Bailey: ‘Keep saying funny things – that’s quite profound advice’
  • Stuffed into a shoebox, seized by the FBI: the amazing fates of Hollywood’s greatest dresses
  • Anna bought abortion pills via social media. Now, like thousands of other Filipinas, she is dead
  • Xi Jinping in Covid scare during Hong Kong handover trip
  • Game of stones: calls to protect Spanish city’s centuries-old noughts and crosses
  • Call to tighten New Zealand law that allows public pooing if no one watching
  • Surviving Britain’s homelessness crisis – podcast
  • China calls on Myanmar junta to hold talks with opponents
  • Copenhagen shooting: suspect charged after three killed in Denmark shopping centre attack
  • Women’s Euro 2022 team guide No 15: Iceland
  • ‘I’m really feeling the squeeze’: single mothers on the living costs crisis
  • Australian PM visits sites of Russian atrocities in Ukraine
  • Liz Truss mulls seizure of Russian assets in UK to give to Ukraine
  • Zhou Guanyu says halo device saved his life after British GP horror crash
  • Eddie Jones to stick with England’s Smith-Farrell axis for Australia rematch
  • ‘I’m the last one standing’: Norrie happy to carry British hopes at Wimbledon
  • Cheteshwar Pujara delights in soaking up England attack in fifth Test | Andy Bull
  • Johnson faces backlash for ‘failure to act’ over Chris Pincher warnings
  • Jonny Bairstow indispensable to England after so long as spare part | Jonathan Liew
  • Nicola Jennings on claims Boris Johnson was warned about Chris Pincher – cartoon
  • Akron police release body-cam video in fatal shooting of Jayland Walker
  • 10-year-old rape victim forced to travel from Ohio to Indiana for abortion
  • Two women killed in shark attacks in Egypt’s Red Sea
  • ‘Knowledge is power’: how to decode skincare ingredients
  • Russia says it has full control of Luhansk region in Ukraine
  • India bars Pulitzer-winning Kashmiri photojournalist from flying to France
  • Dylan Groenewegen enjoys redemption to win stage three of Tour de France
  • Liz Cheney won’t rule out criminal referral against Donald Trump
  • The Railway Children Return review – family classic sequel stays on track
  • ‘TurkAegean’ tourism campaign draws angry response from Athens
  • JMW Turner and sex: exhibition offers insight into private life of artist
  • I’ve had crushes all my life, but I’m getting married soon – and making a promise to love just one person | Nell Frizzell
  • Cristiano Ronaldo’s departure will hurt but now Manchester United can move forward | Jonathan Wilson
  • Goodbye silicone? A new era of breast reconstruction is on the horizon
  • Fresh row as Israel to conduct forensic tests on bullet that killed Shireen Abu Aqleh
  • Women’s rights have suffered a grim setback. But history is still on our side | Rebecca Solnit
  • Peter Brook: the great seeker of British theatre
  • Britain’s Notre Dame? Why MPs need to solve the Houses of Parliament problem
  • ‘Each day is agony’: the lives swept up in El Salvador’s mass arrests
  • ‘People want me dead’: abortion providers fear violence after Roe overturned
  • Double take: the weird and wonderful world of celebrity lookalikes
  • Actor David Earl: ‘Sex scenes? I’d rather go back to gardening’
  • Yes, Nato has a new vitality. But its united front could collapse when it has to deal with Russia | Jonathan Eyal
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 4 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • GST hike should not be pushed back any further, Government prepared to do more if situation ‘worsens significantly’: DPM Wong
  • Woman charged for role as director in suspected shell companies used for money laundering

  • Dollar holds firm as growth fears stalk markets

  • Australia's Link rejects Dye & Durham's lower offer, but keeps door open
  • Malaysia central bank to hike rates again in July and September- Reuters Poll
  • Bezos slams Biden appeal for lower gasoline prices
  • South Korea policymakers to closely monitor impact of rising interest rates

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
